ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific term for sweetened flavoured packaged latte; 'coffee beverage' (FOODON_03301710) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers coffee-based beverages broadly; no specific code exists for sweetened flavoured ready-to-drink latte in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for packaged flavoured latte; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve packaged flavoured latte is sold in a 240 ml can or bottle, equivalent to approximately 240 g, and is consumed as one serving.

nutrient_source.title: Flavoured Latte (Packaged) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Flavoured Latte (Packaged), CR ID D09010028. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for packaged flavoured latte in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 55 (low; GI ≤55 is classified as low per University of Sydney GI Database convention) based on the closest analogous food: sweetened milk-based coffee drink with added sugar. Milk-based beverages with moderate sugar content typically fall in the GI range 45–65. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 29.0) / 100 ≈ 16.0, using a 240 g serving with 12.08 g carbohydrate per 100 g (29.0 g per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: High added sugar content (10.44 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently raises blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this beverage; the 10.44 g/100 g sugar content exceeds the 5 g/100 g SSB threshold and habitual sugar-sweetened beverage consumption is independently associated with elevated blood pressure per AHA 2018 guidelines, regardless of the relatively low sodium content (46 mg/100 g).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 10.44 g sugar/100 g and negligible dietary fibre (0.2 g/100 g) causing rapid postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this packaged flavoured latte; the high sugar load (10.44 g/100 g) combined with negligible fibre (0.2 g/100 g) results in rapid glucose absorption, and liquid sugar from sweetened beverages is associated with the highest glycaemic impact — choose unsweetened plain coffee or an unsweetened latte instead.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar content and potential saturated fat from milk solids and creamers contribute to cardiometabolic risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this packaged flavoured latte; the high added sugar (10.44 g/100 g) contributes to elevated triglycerides and cardiometabolic risk, and palm-based creamers commonly used in packaged latte formulations may add saturated fat — opt for unsweetened black coffee or plain unsweetened latte.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High added sugar promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; flavoured packaged format does not qualify as plain coffee for polyphenol benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this beverage; the 10.44 g/100 g added sugar promotes inflammatory cytokine production, and the flavoured packaged latte format with added sugar and creamers does not confer the anti-inflammatory polyphenol benefit associated with plain unsweetened black coffee.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Flavoured Latte (Packaged) is a commercially manufactured, ready-to-drink sweetened coffee beverage sold in cans or bottles, widely available in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. Known in Chinese as 调味拿铁咖啡（包装）, in Malay as Latte berperisa (bungkusan), in Tamil as சுவையூட்டப்பட்ட லட்டே (பேக்கேஜ்), and in Hindi as स्वादयुक्त लाटे (पैकेज्ड). It is prepared by combining coffee extract or instant coffee with milk or milk solids, added sugar, flavouring agents (such as vanilla, caramel, or hazelnut), and stabilisers, then pasteurised and sealed for ambient or chilled distribution. The beverage has a smooth, creamy texture with a sweet coffee flavour and mild bitterness. Per 100 g, it provides 69 kcal, 3.09 g protein, 1.02 g fat, 12.08 g carbohydrate, and 10.44 g sugar, with only 0.2 g dietary fibre and 46 mg sodium. The high sugar content (10.44 g/100 g) classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB), which is associated with increased risk of type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease with regular consumption. The milk component provides a small amount of protein. This product is ultra-processed and is not suitable for individuals managing diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high added sugar load. It is commonly consumed as a breakfast or snack beverage. Consumers seeking a healthier alternative should choose plain unsweetened latte, black coffee, or kopi o kosong.
common_user_queries: How much sugar is in a packaged latte?
Is canned latte bad for blood pressure?
Is RTD latte suitable for weight loss?
Can I drink flavoured latte if I have hypertension?
Is bottled latte high in sugar?
Flavoured latte vs plain coffee health comparison

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID D09010028) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singapore food records. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate (medium confidence for GI sub-field only) as no direct measurement exists for this specific product. Local-language names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are provided as translations of the canonical English name, consistent with the Singapore commercial market context.
notes: calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 and iron_mg is reported as 0.0 in the HPB source record; these are confirmed zero values per the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for D09010028, not missing data. saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.6 g/100 g based on the fat profile typical of milk-based packaged coffee beverages (approximately 60% of total fat as saturated fat); this is an approximation as the HPB source does not disaggregate fat fractions for this entry. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient, and moisture data for this packaged flavoured latte are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry D09010028 as of 2026. GI value of 55 is classified as low per the University of Sydney GI Database convention (GI ≤55 = low); gi_category corrected from 'medium' to 'low' accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate based on sweetened milk-based coffee beverages; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. This food is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) and is not recommended for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, rheumatoid arthritis, or cardiovascular disease. The dietary tag 'low_calorie' from the input record is noted but considered potentially misleading given the high sugar content; it has been omitted from health guidance. Local-language names (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) are provided as market-appropriate translations for Singapore commercial context; these are translation equivalents of the canonical English name rather than culturally distinct dish names.

improvement_actions_taken: Added Chinese simplified (调味拿铁咖啡（包装）), Chinese traditional (調味拿鐵咖啡（包裝）), Malay (Latte berperisa (bungkusan)), Tamil (சுவையூட்டப்பட்ட லட்டே (பேக்கேஜ்)), and Hindi (स्वादयुक्त लाटे (पैकेज्ड)) to local_names to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance.
Added all five local-language name strings to the flat aliases array and to aliases_by_language under their respective language keys to satisfy Q8 and the aliases consistency rule.
Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' — GI of 55 is classified as low per University of Sydney GI Database convention (GI ≤55 = low), fixing the Q4 failure from iteration 2.
Updated gi_source.source_match_notes to document the GI classification convention.
Updated llm_training.natural_language_description to include local-language names for multilingual retrieval support.
Updated confidence_reason and notes to reflect the addition of local-language names and the gi_category correction.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
